mechanism: Personification and spatial geometry. Walls literally meet at the corner of a room, which doubles as the colloquial phrase for humans meeting at a street corner.

en.setup: What did one wall say to the other?
en.punchline: I'll meet you at the corner!
en.combined: What did one wall say to the other? I'll meet you at the corner!
en.portability_notes: Classic personification dad joke. Works entirely on the geometric reality of walls.

fr.setup: Que dit un mur à un autre mur ?
fr.combined: Que dit un mur à un autre mur ? On se retrouve au coin !
fr.portability_notes: 'Au coin' works perfectly for both a street corner and the corner of a room.

es.setup: ¿Qué le dice una pared a otra pared?
es.combined: ¿Qué le dice una pared a otra pared? ¡Nos vemos en la esquina!
es.portability_notes: 'Esquina' perfectly translates the dual meaning of a structural corner and a meeting point.

ja.setup: 一つの壁がもう一つの壁に何て言った？
ja.combined: 一つの壁がもう一つの壁に何て言った？「角で会おう！」
ja.portability_notes: '角' (kado) correctly means corner. Meeting at the corner translates naturally without relying on Japanese-specific puns (oyaji gyagu), while maintaining the groan-inducing personification.
